seoTitle: Make an Employee Inactive in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Offboard staff cleanly in QuickBooks Online by marking them inactive without losing historical payroll or tax records.
intro: Offboarding a staff member shouldn't mean erasing their payroll history. This guide shows how to mark an employee inactive in QuickBooks Online so they're archived out of your active directory while preserving compliance records.

steps.heading: Open the employee directory
steps.body: Go to Payroll, then Employees, to see your full staff list.

steps.heading: Open the employee's profile
steps.body: Click on the specific employee's name to open their profile setup panel.

steps.heading: Edit employment details
steps.body: Find the Employment details section and click the Edit pencil icon.

steps.heading: Set the status to inactive
steps.body: Open the Employment Status dropdown and choose Inactive or Terminated, then enter their last day of work.

steps.heading: Save and verify
steps.body: Click Save or Done, then check the Status filter above your employee grid to confirm the profile now appears under Inactive Employees.

faq.question: Does making an employee inactive delete their payroll history?
faq.answer: No, historical payroll and tax records are preserved even after an employee is marked inactive.

faq.question: Can I still see inactive employees in my directory?
faq.answer: Yes, use the status filter above the employee grid and select Inactive Employees to view archived profiles.

faq.question: Do I need to enter a reason for the status change?
faq.answer: It's optional, but choosing a reason from the dropdown helps maintain a clear HR log for future reference.

faq.question: Can I reactivate an employee later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can reopen their profile and change the Employment Status field back to Active if needed.
